q: Pineapple on pizza: yes or no?

e.0: You're in team adventurous. The sweet-salty contrast is a feature, not a bug — and you'll defend it against any purist.
e.1: You're a pizza traditionalist. Tomato, mozzarella, basil — that's the holy trinity. Fruit on pizza is sacrilege.
e.2: You'll tolerate it on Hawaiian — but otherwise, no. You allow exceptions for context.

q: Coffee or tea in the morning?

e.0: You need a jolt. Black coffee, espresso, or latte — without it your day doesn't start. You judge tea drinkers silently.
e.1: You prefer a softer caffeine curve. Green, black, or matcha — tea fits your slow-morning aesthetic and treats your stomach gently.
e.2: The disciplined type. Water hydrates without crashing — you skip stimulants entirely. Your skin thanks you.

e.0: You crave loyalty and energy — dogs greet you like you've returned from war. Walks become non-negotiable.
e.1: You appreciate independence and elegance. Cats give attention on their terms — and that's enough for you.
e.2: You love all animals — household dynamics decide. Both have their moments.
e.3: You're a plant person, or just don't want the responsibility. Animals are great, just not at your place.

q: Netflix or YouTube on a slow night?

e.0: You want a long-form arc — characters, plot, binge mode. Background-noise series too.
e.1: You start with one video — three hours later you're watching a documentary on submarines. The algorithm owns you.
e.2: You like the real-time vibe. Chat, energy, unscripted moments.
e.3: You scroll 90-second clips until your dopamine is fried. You know it's bad.

e.0: You're in the MCU camp — interconnected stories, jokes, popcorn fun. You've seen most films at least once.
e.1: You prefer the darker tone — Batman, Watchmen, Joker. Substance over wisecracks.
e.2: Superhero fatigue. Too many sequels, too many spandex. You watch other genres.

e.0: You're locked into the ecosystem — Mac, AirPods, iCloud — and you love how it all just works. You pay for the polish.
e.1: You want customization, side-loading, more value. iOS feels like a gilded cage to you.
e.2: Both work. You use whichever's in front of you. No tribe.

e.0: You want sun on your skin and saltwater in your hair. Vacation = horizontal, near a wave.
e.1: You like elevation, cold air, sweating from real exertion. The mountain re-calibrates you.
e.2: Museums, food, walking miles per day, espresso at noon. Cities are your nature.

e.0: You want the smell, the weight, the bookmark. Reading is also a tactile ritual for you.
e.1: You read more since switching. Travel, dim lighting, dictionary built in — Kindle won you over.
e.2: Walking, driving, doing chores — you double up. Your reading hours went up dramatically.
e.3: You meant to read more this year. TikTok and Netflix won. You feel a little guilty about it.

e.0: Commute is dead time, your home setup is better, and you focus deeper alone. You like your dog as a colleague.
e.1: You need people, a real desk, accidental conversations. Home alone makes you stir-crazy.
e.2: Two days at home for focus, three at the office for connection. The best of both.

o: I don't tip if service charge is included 🚫

e.0: American norm — you tip well, knowing servers depend on it. Cultural baseline.
e.1: You meet the average. Above-average for great service, lower for bad — but rarely below 15.
e.2: Strict meritocracy. You don't auto-tip — you calculate based on attentiveness.
e.3: Non-US POV. You won't tip on top of a service charge — already covered.

e.0: You believe in the breakfast religion — protein, carbs, fuel. You're set for hours.
e.1: Coffee is breakfast. Anything else slows you down.
e.2: You eat your first meal around 11am — by definition brunch. You like the slow morning.
e.3: You don't eat before noon — feels lighter, mental clarity sharper. Lab work backs you up.

e.0: You're up before everyone, get your most important work done before others wake. You also crash early.
e.1: You're a regular human. Wake up, coffee, work — nothing extreme.
e.2: You do your best work late at night. Mornings are for the broken.

e.0: You're building strength, mass, definition. The barbell is your therapist.
e.1: You need the cardio high — the runner's mind clearing. Easier to do anywhere.
e.2: You want everything in 30 minutes. Short, intense, brutal — and out.
e.3: You prioritize flexibility, breathing, mind-body. Less aggressive, equally effective.

e.0: You want one artist, focused performance, comfortable venue. Quality over chaos.
e.1: You want the camping, the strangers becoming friends, the 3-day high. Coachella, Glastonbury, you know the drill.
e.2: Crowds drain you. You'll see live music in a small bar with 50 people, not 50,000.

e.0: You collect, you flip records, you listen full albums. Streaming feels disposable.
e.1: You live in Discover Weekly. You'd be lost without your algorithm.
e.2: You want the sound quality, the Apple integration. You pay for it.
e.3: You like the rare tracks, lives, remixes. You don't want the paywall.

q: Texting reply time: instant or hours?

e.0: You respond fast. You expect the same. Slow replies feel disrespectful.
e.1: You'll reply, just not immediately. You batch checks. People know.
e.2: You'll reply when you're ready — could be days. You don't owe instant access.

q: Honesty or kindness when telling the truth?

e.0: You give it straight. Sugarcoating, in your view, is patronizing.
e.1: You tell the truth — but you wrap it. Same message, different delivery.
e.2: Some truths aren't worth the damage. You'll lie to spare feelings, on small things.

e.0: You protect sleep like a project deadline. You know how much your mood, weight, mind depend on it.
e.1: You're functional but tired. You'd love 8, you get 6-7.
e.2: You hustle, or insomnia got you. You know it's not great but you function.

e.0: You're committed. Your body is a canvas, and the meanings stay with you.
e.1: You went for the test run. A small one, somewhere subtle. You might or might not get more.
e.2: You change your mind too often. Permanent ink scares you. You like temporary.

e.0: Full commitment — ethics, environment, health. You know how to cook the hell out of tofu.
e.1: No meat, but eggs and dairy still in. You feel the difference and stick with it.
e.2: You eat meat sparingly — 1-2 times a week. Mostly plant-based, no rigid rules.
e.3: You love a steak and refuse food guilt. Quality matters — you buy from a good butcher.

e.0: You want the smell of fresh — clothes, shoes, electronics. Used isn't your vibe.
e.1: You love the hunt. Vintage, Depop, eBay — better for the planet and your wallet.
e.2: You buy underwear new, jeans secondhand. It depends on the category.

o: Save a bit, spend a lot 🤷

e.0: Compound interest is your love language. You invest, you have an emergency fund, you talk about it.
e.1: You save some but enjoy life. Balance over discipline.
e.2: You're not building wealth — you're building memories. Or just living mensual.

e.0: You want the ceremony, the ring, the legal-emotional commitment. You've thought about it.
e.1: You don't need a state to validate your love. You'll commit in your own way.
e.2: You're open but not pressed. If it happens organically, great.
e.3: You did it. You stand by it.

e.0: You're sure. Always wanted them. You think about names, schools, the works.
e.1: You see pros and cons. Maybe in 5 years. Time will tell.
e.2: You've decided. Your life, your call. Free time, money, freedom.
e.3: You already know. The chaos and joy of parenthood is your daily life.

q: Dating apps: still using or done?

e.0: You swipe, you go on dates, you make it work. Modern romance.
e.1: You have the app but barely open it. Hope without effort.
e.2: You're burnt out. Quality of matches dropped. You'd rather meet in real life.
e.3: You met your partner the old way — friends, work, chance. Or you're not looking.

o: Yes, if there's an end date 📅
Done it, hard but worth it 💕

e.0: Doable, but you need a horizon. Otherwise it's an open-ended sentence.
e.1: You want physical presence. Without it, it's not the same.
e.2: You've been through it. It's hard, but you came out stronger — or with great memories.

e.0: You don't care who sees — love is to be lived openly. No shame.
e.1: Hand-holding feels sweet, anything more makes you self-conscious.
e.2: You're discreet by default. Affection is for the privacy of home.

q: Splitting the bill on a date?

e.0: You want equality from minute one. No power dynamic over a dinner.
e.1: You proposed the date, you cover it. It's about consistency, not gender.
e.2: You stick to tradition. Some find it charming, others outdated.

q: Cooking at home or eating out?

e.0: You know what's in your food and you save money. Cooking is a form of care.
e.1: You don't enjoy cooking, restaurants are an experience, delivery is convenience. Worth it.
e.2: You cook weekdays, eat out weekends. Balanced.

e.0: Spreadsheets, reservations, time-blocked days. You don't waste a minute.
e.1: You book flights and hotel — but the rest you figure out on the ground.
e.2: You buy a ticket and go. No itinerary. Higher risk, higher stories.

q: Streaming services: subscribed to how many?

e.0: Netflix, Disney+, HBO Max, Apple TV, Spotify — you pay for everything. You consume a lot.
e.1: You picked the ones you actually use. Avoid bloat.
e.2: You stream from elsewhere. The library can't keep up with your taste. Pirate flag, quietly.

e.0: You want comfort. Steam, relaxation. The cold-shower bros can keep their hype.
e.1: You buy the science — better circulation, alertness. You've made it a habit.
e.2: You compromise. Not too hot, not too cold. Functional.

e.0: You're reliable. You respect people's time. Quick to respond.
e.1: You triage. Urgent stuff gets a same-day reply. Casual stuff waits.
e.2: You forget, get distracted, or just don't feel like it. Friends know you're slow.

e.0: You read 20 reviews, watch a YouTube unboxing. You don't make impulse buys.
e.1: You glance at the average. 4+ stars = OK.
e.2: You buy and see. If it sucks, you return — or learn.

q: Plans canceled at the last minute: relief or annoyance?

e.0: You're an introvert wearing extrovert clothing. A canceled night = a free pajama evening.
e.1: You don't like the disruption. Especially if you turned down other plans.
e.2: Casual hangout = whatever. A concert ticket = drama.

e.0: You don't risk missing a flight. You sit at the gate eating overpriced sushi happily.
e.1: Standard protocol. Enough buffer for security delays.
e.2: You roll dice. Most of the time it works. Sometimes it doesn't.

e.0: You want the buttery feel. Sake nigiri is your default order.
e.1: You want the meatiness without the fat. Akami tuna nigiri.
e.2: You go for the fatty tuna. Pricey, but a special occasion deserves it.

q: Breakfast in bed: yes or messy?

e.0: Croissant crumbs, coffee, sun through curtains. You don't mind the cleanup.
e.1: Crumbs in sheets, possible spillage, weird angles. You eat at the table.
e.2: Once a year, sure. Otherwise no.

q: Texting your ex when you're drunk?

e.0: You've sent the 3am message. The next morning, you stare at the read receipt and want to die.
e.1: You know yourself. You block the temptation before drinking.
e.2: You don't need to. You're past it. Drunk-you is also stable.

q: Friend's wedding: dress up or comfort?

e.0: You take it seriously. Hair, outfit, accessories — you bring your A-game.
e.1: You hit the dress code but keep shoes you can dance in. Smart practical.
e.2: You showed up. Friend doesn't care about your outfit.

q: Birthday parties at 30+: still doing them?

e.0: You love an excuse to gather. Big party, music, full guestlist.
e.1: You age out of big parties — you want a meaningful dinner.
e.2: Birthday fatigue. You skip the planning, do nothing special.

e.0: Sweet-savory salads (chicken-pineapple, feta-watermelon) work for you. Brightness.
e.1: Lettuce is savory. Fruit is dessert. You don't blend them.
e.2: You'll accept it in a Thai or Hawaiian salad — context-dependent.

e.0: You like the sweetness, the slightly softer crunch, the nutritional upgrade.
e.1: Classic, salty, crispy. You don't want sweet in your fries.
e.2: Sometimes you want a meal, sometimes a side. You go with the meal's mood.

e.0: You want pure coffee flavor. Milk dilutes, masks. Espresso style.
e.1: A small softener. You want coffee taste but a smoother feel.
e.2: You're more milk than coffee. You like the texture and the slight bitterness.
e.3: You can't handle caffeine, or you've been told to quit. Decaf is your compromise.

q: Going to bed: phone or book?

e.0: You scroll until your eyes burn. You know it's bad for sleep.
e.1: You read 10 pages — easy fall asleep. Best bedtime habit.
e.2: White noise + light flicker. You can't sleep without it.
e.3: You hit pillow, you're out. Discipline.

q: Group chat: muted or notifications on?

e.0: You batch-read. Too many pings, not worth real-time.
e.1: You engage. Memes, reactions — you live there.
e.2: You couldn't take it. You left silently. Now there's a smaller chat without you.

q: Friends with exes: yes or no?

e.0: You can keep emotions out of it. You and the ex still send memes.
e.1: You believe in clean breaks. Once it's over, it's over.
e.2: If the breakup was civil and time has passed, friendship is fine. If it was messy, no.

e.0: Less is more. White, beige, plants, clean lines. You feel clear in space.
e.1: Every wall has art, books, plants, color. Your home is a personality test.
e.2: Function over form. Things where they ended up. Comfortable, not photo-ready.

q: Drinks at the bar: cocktails or beer?

e.0: You like the craft, the variety, the small theater of a good bartender.
e.1: You want simple, cold, reliable. Beer with a pint glass.
e.2: You're at a wine bar more than a beer bar. Glass of red or white, ideally natural.
e.3: You don't drink, or you're not drinking tonight. You order a soda or a mocktail.

q: Solo travel: love it or avoid it?

e.0: You think clearly alone. You meet random people. You eat what you want, when you want.
e.1: You did it once. Was OK. But you missed sharing the moments.
e.2: You haven't, but you're curious — or actively against. Loneliness travels with you.

q: Movie at the cinema: still going?

e.0: You love the theater experience. Big screen, sound, darkness. You see it all.
e.1: For Marvel, Nolan, the big spectacles. Not for indie or drama.
e.2: Cinema can't compete with your couch and a streaming subscription.

e.0: Coffee + news. You feel informed. You vote, you discuss.
e.1: You read titles. Enough to know, not enough to spiral.
e.2: You decided it's bad for you. Headlines stress you out. You'll catch up if it matters.

e.0: You drafted this answer with it. It's your second brain. You've increased your output 2x.
e.1: You use it for one-offs — complex emails, code stuck, recipe ideas. Not your main tool.
e.2: You distrust it — accuracy, jobs displacement, ethics. You use Google.

q: How early do you arrive to dinner with friends?

e.0: You don't want to be the first. Buffer time for last looks.
e.1: You're punctual. You respect time. Others should too.
e.2: You leave early, traffic was light, now you're sitting at the bar alone. Awkward but committed.

e.0: You go shopping with intention. A wrapped box still means something to you.
e.1: You believe memories beat objects. You gift a class, a trip, a dinner.
e.2: Honest and direct. They know best what they want.

e.0: You buy less, you buy better. Patagonia, Veja, secondhand. Higher price, less guilt.
e.1: You want trends now. Zara, H&M, Shein. You wear, you toss.
e.2: Basics from sustainable brands, trends from fast fashion. Compromise.

q: Restaurant: try new place or favorite spot?

e.0: You hate going twice. The world has thousands of restaurants, you want to try them.
e.1: You go to the same Italian every two weeks. They know your order. You're content.
e.2: You alternate. Some weeks adventurous, some weeks home base.

q: Smoothies or solid food for breakfast?

e.0: Fast, packed with nutrients, easy in the morning rush. You blend.
e.1: You need to chew. Eggs, toast, bowl. Smoothies feel like dessert.
e.2: Not hungry until noon. You can't force it.

e.0: Wins the day. Empty gym, mental clarity, energy boost.
e.1: Lunch break workout — quick, efficient, ready for the afternoon.
e.2: You wake up slowly. Evening = peak energy. Crowded gym, but it's your time.

q: Listening to podcasts: yes or no?

e.0: You commute, run, cook with one in your ear. You're a podcast junkie.
e.1: Specific episodes when recommended. Not a constant habit.
e.2: Music is your soundtrack. Podcasts feel like work.

q: Reading the book or watching the movie first?

e.0: Books are richer. The movie will only enhance, not replace.
e.1: Watch the simpler version, then dive deeper.
e.2: Both stand alone. You consume whichever crosses your path.

e.0: You want companionship that follows you around. Walks, training, the works.
e.1: You want a roommate that judges you. Independent, low-maintenance, royal.
e.2: Tropical fish, parrot, lizard, hedgehog — you love the niche.

e.0: You'd rather pause, control sound, eat your snacks. Streaming has spoiled you.
e.1: You miss the dark room, the giant screen, the crying babies (sometimes).
e.2: Big movies = theater. Indie/dramas = home with a cup of tea.

q: Do you bring a reusable cup or use disposable?

e.0: Sustainability matters daily. Your travel mug travels with you.
e.1: You try, you forget. Partial wins.
e.2: You don't think about it. Single-use is convenient.

q: How many friends in your inner circle?

e.0: Deep > broad. You'd rather have two people who know everything than 20 acquaintances.
e.1: Sweet spot. Variety, but everyone matters.
e.2: Big tribe energy. Parties, big chats, lots going on.

q: Live in big city or small town?

e.0: You want choice, culture, anonymity, hustle. Quiet feels suffocating.
e.1: You want neighbors, space, less noise. The trade is fewer options.
e.2: 30 minutes from the city. Best of both.

e.0: You love being the center for one day. Decorate, gather, dance.
e.1: Smaller, more intimate. Quality time over big show.
e.2: Birthday makes you anxious. You'd rather Netflix.

e.0: You believe it bypasses fees and reaches the server fully.
e.1: Card is your default. Tipping by app is normal.
e.2: Whichever is fastest at checkout. No strong opinion.

e.0: Curry, lasagna, chili — they marinate overnight. You cook for two days.
e.1: Depends. Pasta yes, fish never.
e.2: You hate the texture of reheats. Cook fresh each day.

q: Working out solo or with a friend?

e.0: Headphones in, world out. You don't want to talk during sets.
e.1: Friend keeps you accountable. Plus social time.
e.2: You like the energy of a class — yoga, pilates, spin. Shared intensity.

e.0: You earned it. Sunday is sacred sloth.
e.1: You do laundry, meal prep, organize. Monday starts smooth.
e.2: Hikes, day trips, museums. You can't sit still.

q: Going to bed before or after midnight?

e.0: Sleep is non-negotiable. You protect it ruthlessly.
e.1: You're normal. Some late nights, some early.
e.2: You do your best work late. Mornings are recovery.

q: Splitting rent in a couple: equally or by income?

e.0: Even split feels fair to you, no matter the salary gap.
e.1: Fair = proportional. The higher earner contributes more.
e.2: One partner covers it. Could be an investment in the other's career or convenience.

e.0: 15 minutes a day, no accumulation. Always clean.
e.1: Saturday morning ritual. Vacuum, kitchen, bathroom — done.
e.2: You let it pile. Then a massive day. Cycle repeats.

e.0: Your dog/cat has a chair. They're family.
e.1: They sit nearby and look pathetic. You give scraps occasionally.
e.2: You trained them. They don't beg, they don't come close.

q: Birthday on Instagram: post or not?

e.0: You celebrate publicly. Photos, captions, gratitude.
e.1: A casual story is enough. Visible but low-effort.
e.2: Birthdays are private. You text people who matter.

q: Restaurant reservation: month ahead or walk-in?

e.0: You book months in advance for that hot spot. Worth the planning.
e.1: You decide a few days ahead, book online.
e.2: You walk in. If full, you go somewhere else. Spontaneous.

q: How often do you do laundry?

e.0: You stay on top. Small loads, easy to fold.
e.1: Saturday or Sunday morning. One big batch.
e.2: You wear all clean clothes, then panic on a Sunday night.

e.0: Email, app notifications, paperless statements. Zero paper.
e.1: Some companies still send paper. You deal.
e.2: You like the physical record. Filing cabinet, organized.

e.0: You feel a midday slump. Sugar fixes it. You know it crashes you later.
e.1: Salty / crunchy is your tension release. Trail mix or chips.
e.2: You're disciplined. Snack = energy, not pleasure.
e.3: You don't snack. Three meals and that's it.

q: Holiday season weight gain: stressed or OK?

e.0: You monitor closely. December is hard. January is for reset.
e.1: You enjoy the food, no guilt. Mid-January you go back to routine.
e.2: You don't think about it. Body changes are part of life.

q: Wearing the same outfit twice in a week?

e.0: You curate. Different days, different outfits. Photos preserve uniqueness.
e.1: If no one important saw, you might re-wear. Pragmatic.
e.2: You wear what works. Repeating a great outfit is a feature, not a bug.

q: Talking on the phone: love or hate?

e.0: You're a voice person. Faster than text, more human.
e.1: You take calls from family, partner, close friends only. Anyone else = text.
e.2: Phone calls give you anxiety. You read a text 5x before reading a voicemail once.

e.0: You want your hair color from your 30s back. You dye monthly.
e.1: You let it happen. Salt and pepper looks good — or you go full gray with pride.
e.2: You barely think about it. Hair is hair.

q: At a party: dance floor or back porch?

e.0: You're there for the movement. Music drives you.
e.1: Deep talks beat dancing. You're in a corner with one or two people.
e.2: You're stationed near the food. Light social, easy escape.

q: Saying 'I love you' first: yes or wait?

e.0: You don't hide. If you feel it, you say it. The risk is part of the deal.
e.1: You wait. Letting the other take the leap feels safer.
e.2: You believe in synchronicity. You wait for the right moment, ideally simultaneous.

q: Phone in bedroom at night: yes or no?

e.0: It's your alarm. Then your last view. Then your first view. You scroll, you know it's wrong.
e.1: Phone is on the dresser. You force yourself to get up to silence it.
e.2: Bedroom is sacred. Phone stays in the kitchen. You sleep like a king.

q: Friends from work: real friends or just colleagues?

e.0: You've been to their weddings, met their kids. Work is where you found family.
e.1: You like them. Lunches happen. But the friendship doesn't survive a job change.
e.2: You keep work and personal life apart. Healthier boundaries.

q: Hot drink at night before bed?

e.0: Chamomile, mint, lavender — a ritual. Wind down.
e.1: Childhood comfort. You sleep better.
e.2: You don't need a wind-down drink. Plain water, lights out.

q: Best part of the holiday: food, gifts, or family?
o: Family time, the rest is bonus 👨‍👩‍👧

e.0: You love big meals, leftovers, indulgence. The rest is decoration.
e.1: You love the surprise — finding the right gift, opening yours. Childhood feeling.
e.2: You see them once or twice a year. The food and gifts come second.

e.0: You keep the peace. You'd rather move on than be right.
e.1: You hold your ground. The other usually breaks.
e.2: You both come back. Healthy.

o: Only if I have my watch ⌚

e.0: You aim for 10k. You're disappointed by 6k.
e.1: When the watch tracks, you notice. Otherwise, you don't.
e.2: You don't quantify movement. You walk, you live.

q: Listening to music while studying or working?

e.0: Background helps your flow — sometimes lyrics, sometimes not.
e.1: You can't have words. Instrumental, classical, lo-fi.
e.2: Anything is noise. Silence + tea.

e.0: Synced, alerts, shared with partner. Modern life.
e.1: You like writing it down. Bullet journal vibes.
e.2: You write what matters in paper, sync the rest in digital.

e.0: You've been. You changed. You'd recommend.
e.1: You think people can solve their own problems with friends and books.
e.2: You think about it. You haven't picked up the phone. Some day.

e.0: You give what they'll use. Less romantic, more useful.
e.1: You spent time finding the perfect thing. They cried.
e.2: Pragmatic. They know best. You give amount, they decide.
